person.name: John Dunbar of Moray - 1st of Mochrum

nameForms.fullText: John Dunbar of Moray - 1st of Mochrum

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Life of John DUNBAR«/b»
Sir John Dunbar was also known as Sir John Dunbar (of Mochrum) in records. He was born in 1445 in Scotland. He was the son of Sir Alexander Dunbar and Isobel Sutherland.

Sir John Dunbar married Margaret Dunbar (Dunbar), daughter of Patrick Dunbar, before 21 June 1474 in Scotland. By his marriage to Margaret, the second daughter of Patrick Dunbar of Mochrum, he obtained the lands of Mochrum Park. their descendants held Mochrum, Baldoon & Grange.

Sir John Dunbar married secondly Janet Stewart after 1483. 

12 Sep 1502 he obtained for himself and his heirs a nine year's grant of the office of Steward of Kirkcudbright and Keeper of Thrieve Castle. With this building he acquired the lands of Thrieve Grange, the fisheries of the river Dee, and the revenues belonging to the Castle, for which he engaged to pay the King £100 yearly, and to keep the fortlet at his own expense. In the following year he was killed by Alexander Gordon, younger of Lochinvar, which occasioned a feud for many years afterwards.

John died in 1503 in Mochrum, Wigtownshire, Scotland. He was killed by Alexander, the heir apparent of Gordon of Lochinvar. He was designated of Mochrum, Stewartry of Kirkcudbright. This line had Mochrum, Baldoon & Grange.

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Dunbar of Moray - 1st of Mochrum於大約1448年出生在英國蘇格蘭度福利斯 安德 加洛韋維托時，他的父親Sir Alexander Dunbar of Moray, of Westfield - Sheriff of Moray29歲，母親Isobel Sutherland20歲。他於1470年之前與Janet Stewart結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1503年在英國蘇格蘭度福利斯 安德 加洛韋莫申盧姆享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: Dunbar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Dunbar, a place on the North Sea coast near Edinburgh, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’ + barr ‘top, summit’.

History: A family of this name were granted the lands of Dunbar by Malcolm III (1058–1093), and later acquired the earldoms of Dunbar and March. These earldoms were then withdrawn from the family by James I in 1434.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When John Dunbar of Moray - 1st of Mochrum was born about 1448, in Wigtown, Dumfries and Galloway, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Alexander Dunbar of Moray, of Westfield - Sheriff of Moray, was 29 and his mother, Isobel Sutherland, was 20. He married Janet Stewart before 1470. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died about 1503, in Mochrum, Dumfries and Galloway, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 57.

ko.lifeSummary: John Dunbar of Moray - 1st of Mochrum(이)가 대략 1448년 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리스 앤드 갈로아이 위그타운에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Alexander Dunbar of Moray, of Westfield - Sheriff of Moray(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Isobel Sutherland(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1470년 이전 Janet Stewart(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1503년 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리스 앤드 갈로아이 모크럼에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Dunbar of Moray - 1st of Mochrum родился около 1448 г. в местности вигтоун, думфриесь анд галлоуеи, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Alexander Dunbar of Moray, of Westfield - Sheriff of Moray было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Isobel Sutherland, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Janet Stewart перед 1470 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер около 1503 г. в местности мочрум, думфриесь анд галлоуеи, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 57 лет.
